title: Blank page
testCase: Is there at least one heading and one paragraph on the page?
blocks.0.text: The editor on the right is empty. There is nothing. Nothing to fix, no dashes to fill in, no code to copy.
blocks.1.text: This is the moment where most people freeze. You know everything you need — and still your fingers will not move. That is normal. You will live through it many more times.
blocks.3.text: No instructions. No steps. No right answer. Just this: a page that says who you are and what you do.
blocks.4.text: You know two tags. They are enough.
blocks.6.text: Write anything at all. A name. A single letter. Just do not stare at the empty screen. Code gets written by starting to write — not by sitting and wondering what the right way is.
blocks.7.text: Other courses teach you to follow steps. This lesson teaches you to start from nothing. The second is the hard part, and the second is what you will do for the rest of your life.
